senses.definition: (of a building, car, etc.) left and not needed or used anymore

examples.x: The abandoned house was falling apart.
They found an abandoned puppy on the street.
An abandoned suitcase lay in the empty station.

senses.definition: related to the language, literature, art, or culture of ancient Rome and Greece

examples.x: The course covered classical philosophy, examining the ideas of Socrates and Plato.
She specializes in classical literature, focusing on works by Homer and Virgil.
The university offers courses in classical languages like Latin and Ancient Greek.
The museum’s exhibit features classical sculptures from ancient Greece.

senses.definition: located on the outer surface of something

examples.x: The building’s external walls were insulated to improve energy efficiency.
She used an external microphone to capture high-quality audio for her podcast recordings.
She inspected the car for any external damage after the accident.
The external surface of the container was coated to prevent rust.

senses.definition: related to the manufacturing or production of goods on a large scale

examples.x: The city's economy thrived due to its industrial base, which included manufacturing plants and factories.
Industrial machinery is used to automate production processes in factories.
Environmental regulations aim to mitigate the impact of industrial activities on air and water quality.
Industrial design focuses on creating products that are both functional and aesthetically pleasing.

senses.definition: (of buildings or rooms) having few or no internal walls, creating a large, open space

examples.x: Our office moved to a new open-plan space to encourage collaboration among employees.
The apartment has an open-plan kitchen and living area, perfect for entertaining guests.
The school's library was redesigned into an open-plan layout to accommodate more students.
The open-plan design of the restaurant allows diners to see into the kitchen while they eat.

senses.definition: (of a room, house, etc.) large with a lot of space inside
to build a house, bridge, machine, etc.

examples.x: Engineers and construction workers collaborated to construct a sturdy and safe bridge.
The company is planning to construct a new office building to accommodate its growing workforce.
Skilled carpenters were hired to construct custom-made furniture for the luxury hotel.
Architects designed the blueprint, and construction crews worked together to construct the modern skyscraper.
To improve transportation, the city decided to construct a new subway system.

senses.definition: a block of baked clay, mostly used to build houses, walls, etc.

examples.x: The walls of the house were built with red bricks, giving it a classic look.
They used brick to create a sturdy fireplace in the living room.
The old building was made of weathered bricks, showing its age.
He learned how to lay bricks as part of his training in construction.

senses.definition: a vertical structural element, often made of stone, that supports the weight of the building above it

examples.x: The ancient Greek temples are known for their majestic columns.
The Roman architecture featured grand columns supporting large arches.
The interior of the cathedral was adorned with ornate columns carved from marble.
The architect designed the modern office building with sleek, minimalist columns.
The museum's entrance was framed by towering columns, adding to its grandeur.

senses.definition: a hard material used for building structures, made by mixing cement, water, sand, and small stones

examples.x: The workers poured concrete to create a sturdy foundation for the house.
The sidewalk was made of concrete, providing a durable surface for pedestrians.
She designed a beautiful patio using colored concrete for an attractive finish.
They added rebar to the concrete for extra reinforcement.
The construction project involved a large amount of concrete for various structures.

senses.definition: a piece of land that new buildings are being built or are planned to be built, often with the purpose of urban expansion or improvement
a machine used for digging earth

examples.x: The digger efficiently removed the dirt to make way for the foundation.
He operated the digger with precision to create a deep trench.
The construction crew rented a large digger for the project.
He learned how to use the digger during his summer job at the construction site.

senses.definition: a narrow hallway that provides access to rooms inside a building or between other buildings

examples.x: The passage led to a series of small offices on the second floor.
She walked down the dark passage to reach the library.
The old mansion had a hidden passage that connected to the garden.
The museum featured a long passage displaying art from various periods.

senses.definition: a part of a road through which vehicles can move on to another

examples.x: She took the exit to the city center to reach her destination.
The highway exit was clearly marked with signs indicating the direction to the airport.
He missed the exit and had to drive several miles before finding another way back.
The GPS instructed them to take the next exit to reach their hotel.

senses.definition: a small simple house or shelter that usually has only one room

examples.x: The family lived in a small hut made of bamboo and thatch.
The travelers took refuge in a hut while waiting for the storm to pass.
The fishing village was dotted with colorful huts along the shore.
She built a cozy hut in the forest for her weekend getaways.
They found an abandoned hut during their hike in the mountains.

senses.definition: to destroy a building, area, etc. completely

examples.x: The demolition crew leveled the old factory to make way for the new development project.
The hurricane leveled entire neighborhoods, leaving behind a path of destruction.
The controlled explosion leveled the condemned building, clearing the space for urban renewal.
Wildfires swept through the forest, leveling acres of trees and wildlife habitats.
The bombing raid leveled residential areas, leaving civilians displaced and homeless.

senses.definition: to build something once again, after it has been destroyed or severely damaged

examples.x: After the hurricane, the town worked hard to rebuild the damaged homes.
The organization aims to rebuild communities affected by natural disasters.
The city plans to rebuild the old bridge using modern materials.
The school district received funding to rebuild the playground.
The architect was hired to rebuild the historic site according to its original design.

senses.definition: (plural) the remains of something such as a building after it has been seriously damaged or destroyed

examples.x: They explored the ruins of an old castle during their trip.
The ancient ruins attracted many tourists each year.
The archaeologists worked to preserve the historical ruins.
They took photographs of the beautiful ruins in the sunset.
The archaeological team discovered the ruins of an ancient city.

senses.definition: the raised edge at the side of a street, usually made of stone

examples.x: The city installed a new curb to improve pedestrian safety.
The construction crew repaired the damaged curb after the winter storms.
A high curb can pose a challenge for people with mobility issues.
The curb along the street was painted to enhance visibility at night.

senses.definition: a piece of land under which waste material is buried

examples.x: The city opened a new landfill to accommodate its growing waste management needs.
Trash is transported to the landfill for safe disposal.
The old landfill site is being transformed into a park.
Regulations require that all waste be sorted before it goes to the landfill.
Many communities are working to reduce the amount of waste sent to the landfill.

senses.definition: a system of underground pipes and tunnels used to carry away used water and waste matter from houses, factories, etc.

examples.x: Raw sewage leaked into the river due to a malfunction in the sewer network.
Urban planners designed the new neighborhood with a modern sewer to accommodate population growth.
The sewer inspector checked for cracks and leaks in the aging infrastructure to prevent contamination.

senses.definition: a structure or a place that is historically important

examples.x: The Great Wall of China is a remarkable landmark that stretches across thousands of miles and has stood for centuries.
The Colosseum in Rome, once used for gladiator contests, remains a significant landmark from ancient times.
The Taj Mahal in India, with its stunning white marble architecture, is a universally recognized landmark.
In Washington, D.C., the Lincoln Memorial serves as both a tribute to President Lincoln and a powerful landmark of American history.

senses.definition: a structure built in honor of a public figure or a special event

examples.x: The towering monument was erected in honor of the soldiers who fought bravely in the war.
Tourists flock to the city to see the historic monument that commemorates a significant battle.
The local community raised funds to build a monument dedicated to the town’s founder.
The monument features intricate carvings that tell the story of the event it represents.
Every year, a memorial service is held at the monument to remember those who lost their lives.

senses.definition: a place or a building is designed and equipped for a specific function, such as healthcare, education, etc.

examples.x: The hospital's new facility includes state-of-the-art operating rooms and patient care units.
The factory expanded its production capacity with a new manufacturing facility.
The community center is a multi-purpose facility offering classes, sports programs, and events.
The school district built a new educational facility to accommodate growing enrollment.

senses.definition: a place where people play and bet their money on gambling games

examples.x: The casino was filled with the sounds of slot machines and excited players.
They planned a weekend trip to the casino to celebrate their anniversary.
The luxurious casino featured high-stakes poker tables and gourmet restaurants.
Many people visit the casino hoping to win big jackpots.
The casino hosted a special event with live music and entertainment.

senses.definition: a building containing judicial courts, offices of judges, etc.

examples.x: She filed for divorce at the local courthouse.
The historic courthouse has been preserved as a landmark.
The courthouse security tightened ahead of the high-profile trial.
The new courthouse features modern amenities and accessible facilities.

senses.definition: a place or party at which people dance to music

examples.x: The disco was packed with people dancing to the latest hits.
She wore a sparkly dress to the disco to stand out on the dance floor.
He loved going to the disco every weekend to socialize with friends.
The disco offered special promotions on drinks for early arrivals.

senses.definition: a private institute where old people live and are taken care of

examples.x: The nursing home offers around-the-clock medical supervision for its residents.
Many families choose a nursing home when they can no longer care for their elderly loved ones at home.
Activities such as bingo and arts and crafts are organized regularly at the nursing home.
Staff members at the nursing home are trained to handle various health conditions.
The nursing home features comfortable rooms and communal areas for socializing.

senses.definition: a small building, often in a village, that is used as a school

examples.x: Children gather every morning at the schoolhouse for lessons.
The schoolhouse is equipped with modern technology for interactive learning.
Community meetings are sometimes held in the old schoolhouse.
The schoolhouse had a bell that rang to signal the beginning and end of classes.

senses.definition: anything that is built from several parts, such as a house, bridge, etc.

examples.x: The Eiffel Tower in Paris is an iconic structure made of iron lattice.
Engineers design the structure of skyscrapers to withstand wind and seismic forces.
A beehive is a remarkable natural structure created by bees.
The architect designed a sustainable structure using environmentally friendly materials.
The ancient Roman aqueduct is an impressive structure that spans several kilometers.

senses.definition: a building in which the officials of a town work

examples.x: The mayor held a meeting at the town hall to discuss community concerns.
The town hall serves as a hub for local government activities.
The town hall is also used for community events and celebrations.
Citizens can access public records at the town hall.
Local elections are supervised at the town hall.

senses.definition: a place where dead bodies are prepared in order to be buried or burned

examples.x: The family chose a local funeral home for their loved one's services.
The funeral home helped arrange the details of the memorial ceremony.
The staff at the funeral home provided compassionate support to the grieving family.
A viewing was held at the funeral home for friends and family to gather.
The funeral home offered various options for caskets and urns.

senses.definition: a piece of land where dead people are buried, often situated near a church

examples.x: The old graveyard was filled with weathered headstones.
They walked through the graveyard to pay their respects to their ancestors.
The graveyard was peaceful, surrounded by tall trees and birds singing.
Flowers were placed on each grave in the graveyard during the memorial service.
She often visited the graveyard to reflect on her loved ones' lives.

senses.definition: an overground or underground grave that is large in size and is often made of stone

examples.x: They placed flowers at the entrance of the tomb to honor their loved one.
Historians studied the artifacts found in the tomb for clues about the past.
Visitors came from far and wide to see the famous royal tomb.
The tomb was sealed to protect the remains inside from damage.
